location.office_time_required: regular in-office presence in San Francisco

level_signals: Expected to set strategy, publish roadmap, and operate without management oversight

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You bring ~8+ years of product experience, including time owning an enterprise segment and/or compliance or governance at a technical product company.
including time owning an enterprise segment and/or compliance or governance at a technical product company.
You've built product at a technical company where the buyers included legal, security, compliance, and procurement.
You speak fluent API and fluent enterprise procurement. Both. In the same meeting.
You've been in a security review, a BAA negotiation, and a legal hold conversation.
You understand that for regulated industries, governance isn't a nice-to-have — and you're hungry to build more.
You have a strong point of view on what AI means for the enterprise, and you've already been building with it.
You don't need a manager to tell you what matters. You see the problem, build the plan, ship, and report back with results.
You like hanging out regularly with Karl the Fog in our San Francisco office.

scope.surface: Enterprise & Governance product layer — trust infrastructure including live guardrails, audit trails, monitoring, and compliance for voice AI
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS, developer platform with enterprise upsell
primary_metrics_inferred: Time-to-close on enterprise deals blocked by compliance gaps
core_responsibilities: Map where enterprise deals stall: legal, security, compliance reviews, feature gaps, onboarding
Write a 'here's the mess + here's the opportunity' brief for the enterprise segment
Own and publish a 12-month rolling roadmap for enterprise
Deliver a step-function improvement in a core enterprise metric
Raise the bar across Vapi by demonstrating what great product management looks like

green_flags: Clear mission and rationale for the hire — enterprise revenue 5×'ed, specific problem articulated
Explicit 30/60/90 day plan demonstrates organizational clarity and onboarding investment
Strong equity culture mentioned (70% previous founders)
Tier-1 investors (YC, KP, Bessemer) signal credibility and growth trajectory
$10k annual L&D budget is a concrete perk signal
Direct statement that 'This person will be critical to the success of the company' signals high organizational visibility
red_flags: Onsite San Francisco requirement may limit candidate pool; framed colloquially ('Karl the Fog') but is a hard location requirement
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization
Principal PM role with no direct reports — high expectations with no stated structural support
Phrase 'zero hand-holding required' may signal under-resourced or sink-or-swim culture

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level inferred from '~8+ years' and 'Principal PM' framing in body text
location.type inferred as onsite from 'hanging out regularly... in our San Francisco office'
scope.business_model inferred from product description and enterprise focus
scope.primary_metrics_inferred are inferences from responsibilities, not stated in JD
compensation.source set to unknown as no figures are provided
ambiguities: The JD uses the word 'Principal PM' in the body ('We need a Principal PM') but the role title is 'Product Manager - Enterprise & Governance Agents' — seniority level labeled as Principal PM based on body text
Location is described only as 'our San Francisco office' with a cultural reference; no explicit policy on remote or hybrid work stated — treated as onsite
~8+ years uses a tilde, suggesting flexibility; treated as hard minimum but flagged as approximate
